channel.title: Governance on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Governance on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed settings now support bypass permission controls
item.description:
Enterprise administrators can now set disableBypassPermissionsMode to "disable" in enterprise-managed settings.json to prevent GitHub Copilot CLI and VS Code from bypassing permission controls.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Policy Coverage for Model Router in Foundry Models
item.description:
Azure Policy now supports centralized governance for deployments that use the model router in Foundry Models. This lets organizations define and enforce routing standards to align model selection with security, compliance, and operational requirements.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: commandline: Disposable agents, durable memory: The architecture behind Squad
item.description:
The article describes Squad, a file-backed agent-team architecture built around human oversight. It emphasizes inspectable memory, explicit orchestration, and moving governance rules from prompts into code.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft Security success stories: How St. Luke’s and ManpowerGroup are securing AI foundations
item.description:
Microsoft highlights how St. Luke’s and ManpowerGroup are using governance, identity, and cloud security controls to support AI adoption at scale. The post frames security as part of the foundation for AI deployment rather than a separate concern.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: dotnet: Announcing Agent Governance Toolkit MCP Extensions for .NET
item.description:
Microsoft announced a public preview .NET package for MCP servers that adds governance features through a single builder extension. The package includes policy enforcement, startup tool scanning, fallback governance, and response sanitization.

  • Source: .NET
